name: Standing Cross-Leg Lateral Stretch
description: Standing QL and lateral-line release. Cross one foot over in front of the other so the legs are scissored, then reach the same-side arm overhead and lean the torso AWAY from the crossed (back) leg — the stretch runs down the side of the trunk into the quadratus lumborum and the lateral hip of the back leg. Hold, breathe into the stretched side, then switch the leg cross and lean the other way. User-discovered (W12D5): crossing the legs and leaning side to side loosens a tight QL/psoas better than a square-stance side bend, because the leg cross pre-tensions the lateral hip so the lean reaches the QL directly. Common cue: 'scissor the legs, reach long overhead, lean away from the back leg — feel it from the hip up the side of the waist.' Slow, no bouncing, ~30s each side.
